NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH THAT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 7 MILES FROM 1 S OF DONALDSON TO 1 N OF PLYMOUTH. THIS IS A WESTWARD EXTENSION OF THE PREVIOUS TRACK AND INCREASE IN INTENSITY. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH ONE RESIDENCE SUFFERING MAJOR ROOF LOSS AND OTHER STRUCTURAL DAMAGE.
TREE DAMAGE REPORTED AROUND PINE LAKE

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH THAT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1.3 MILES FROM 2 WNW OF PLYMOUTH TO 1 N OF PLYMOUTH. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 95 MPH THAT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.2 MILES. SEVERE TREE DAMAGE AND CONSIDERABLE STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO A HOME OCCURRED. A GARAGE WAS DESTROYED FROM A LARGE UPROOTED TREE. NEARBY BARN BUILDING WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION.
TREES BLOWN DOWN ALONG GREENLAWN NEAR THE ZOO.
3 TREE LIMBS DOWN AT HOME. ONE LANDED ON CAR

12 TREES DOWN FROM BERRIEN SPRINGS TO STEVENSVILLE. TIME ESTIMATED.
BETWEEN NAPPANEE AND WAKARUSA WEST OF SR 19. 6 TO 8 INCH DIAMETER LIMBS AND UTILITY LINES DOWN. CORN CROP LAYED OVER

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 95 MPH AND A PATH LENGTH OF 0.6 MILES. TORNADO DAMAGED OCCURRED ON EDGE OF LARGER SWATH OF WIND DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS UP TO 18 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE SNAPPED WITH ROUGHLY 6 UPROOTED TREES. A BARN SUFFERED EXTENSIVE ROOF AND WALL DAMAGE.
DAMAGE FOUND CONSISTENT WITH A EF0 TORNADO. TOUCHDOWN WEST OF MILFORD. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A STORAGE SHED AND BARN. CROP DAMAGE IN AREA. MAX WINDS 80 MPH. LENGTH 1.9 MILES.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH THAT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 7 MILES FROM 2.8 SSW OF SILVER LAKE IN NORTHWEST WABASH COUNTY TO 6.9 ESE OF SILVER LAKE IN FAR SOUTHERN KOSCIUSKO COUNTY. EXTENSIVE TREE AND CROP DAMAGE WAS NOTED. SEVERAL FARMSTEADS ALONG THE PATH SUSTAINED MINOR TO MODERATE STRUCTURE DAMAGE.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED.

*** 1 INJ *** TREE FELL ONTO SOUTH COUNTY ROAD...WEST OF COUNTY ROAD 43. 61 YR OLD MALE DRIVER ENCOUNTERED THE TREE AND ATTEMPTED TO SWERVE TO AVOID. VEHICLE CRASHED INTO DITCH AND SOME TREES. DRIVER SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES.

REPORTS OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY. LARGE TREE ONTO SEVERAL TRAILERS IN FAIRLANE MOBILE HOME PARK.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH THAT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.4 MILES. THIS BRIEF TORNADO DESTORYED A BARN AND CHICKEN COOP AND SCATTERED CONSIDERABLE DEBRIS ONE QUARTER MILE TO THE NORTHEAST WHERE AN ADDITIONAL HORSE BARN LOST MOST OF ITS ROOF AND A NEARBY HOME AND GARAGE HAD MINOR DAMAGE. HOWEVER SEVERE TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED JUST NORTH OF THIS LOCATION WITH SIGNIFICANT DEBRIS LOADING NOTED IN THE TREES...MANY OF WHICH WERE SNAPPED AND OR UPROOTED.
ONE HOME WITH SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE. 15 HOMES WITH MINOR DAMAGE.
